# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Project Overview
Beudox HR is a comprehensive Human Resources management system built as a single-page application (SPA) using React and TypeScript. It provides a complete suite of HR tools including employee management, payroll, evaluations, leave tracking, and project management.

## Architecture & Technology Stack

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes and role-based access control
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Context for global state
- **UI Components**: Shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives with Tailwind CSS
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens

### Backend Architecture
- **Platform**: Supabase (PostgreSQL database + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with migrations for schema management
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with role-based access control
- **API**: RESTful API through Supabase client, with Edge Functions for complex business logic
- **File Storage**: Supabase Storage for documents, avatars, and exports

### Key Design Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into reusable hooks
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for auth, theming, and global state
- **Protected Routes**: Route-level protection with role checking
- **Optimistic Updates**: UI updates before API confirmation for better UX

## Core Business Logic

### Authentication & Authorization
- Multi-role system: CEO, HR Manager, Team Lead, Employee
- Route-level access control with `canAccess()` function
- Password reset and employee invitation flows
- Session management with automatic token refresh

### Employee Management
- CRUD operations for employee records
- Profile management with avatar upload
- Department and role assignments
- Employment status tracking (active/inactive)

### Evaluation System
- **Quarterly Evaluations**: Formal performance reviews with recommendations
- **Daily Evaluations**: Quick feedback system with ratings and comments
- Role-based visibility: Managers see more evaluation details
- Timeline view of evaluation history

### Leave Management
- Multiple leave types (annual, sick, maternity, etc.)
- Balance tracking and automatic calculations
- Approval workflow with notifications
- Calendar integration for holiday planning

### Payroll System
- Automated payroll generation based on attendance and salary data
- Payslip generation and email distribution
- Overtime and allowance calculations
- Tax and deduction handling

### Project & Invoice Management
- Project lifecycle management
- Client relationship tracking
- Invoice generation with PDF export
- Financial reporting and analytics

## Key Files & Directories

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap with React 18 createRoot
- `src/App.tsx`: Main routing configuration and providers setup

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `src/components/ui/`: Shadcn/ui component library (40+ components)

### Business Logic
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control logic
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance calculations and utilities
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification system for alerts and emails
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management

### Pages/Routes
- Authentication: `/login`, `/forgot-password`, `/set-password`
- Dashboard: `/dashboard` (role-specific widgets)
- Employee Management: `/employees/*` (list, profile, forms)
- Evaluations: `/evaluations/*` (quarterly and daily)
- Leave: `/leave` (management interface)
- Projects: `/projects/*` (CRUD operations)
- Invoices: `/invoices/*` (creation and tracking)
- Settings: `/settings` (company configuration)

### Database Schema
- **Core Tables**: employees, companies, departments, roles
- **HR Tables**: evaluations, daily_evaluations, leave_requests, leave_balances
- **Business Tables**: projects, clients, invoices, payroll_records
- **Settings Tables**: company_settings, leave_types, expense_categories

### Supabase Edge Functions
- `supabase/functions/invite-employee/`: Employee invitation with email
- `supabase/functions/generate-payroll/`: Automated payroll calculation
- `supabase/functions/generate-invoice-pdf/`: PDF invoice generation
- `supabase/functions/send-invoice-email/`: Email delivery for invoices
- `supabase/functions/deactivate-employee/`: Employee offboarding

## Development Workflow

### Code Organization
- Feature-based component organization
- Shared UI components in `src/components/ui/`
- Custom hooks for business logic reuse
- Utility functions in `src/lib/`
- Type definitions co-located with components or in separate files

### State Management Strategy
- **Server State**: TanStack Query for API data with caching and synchronization
- **Local State**: React useState/useReducer for component-specific state
- **Global State**: React Context for auth, theme, and app-wide settings
- **Form State**: React Hook Form for complex form management

### Data Fetching Patterns
- Query keys follow hierarchical structure: `['entity', id, 'sub-entity']`
- Optimistic updates for immediate UI feedback
- Background refetching for data freshness
- Error boundaries for graceful failure handling

### Styling Approach
- Utility-first CSS with Tailwind
- Design tokens for consistent theming
- Dark mode support with CSS variables
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

## Common Patterns & Conventions

### Component Patterns
- Functional components with hooks
- TypeScript interfaces for props
- Default exports for page components
- Named exports for reusable components
- Forward refs for form inputs and interactive elements

### Naming Conventions
- PascalCase for components and types
- camelCase for variables, functions, and hooks
- kebab-case for CSS classes and file names
- UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants

### Error Handling
- Try/catch blocks in async operations
- Error boundaries for React component errors
- Toast notifications for user feedback
- Logging for debugging and monitoring

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for utilities and hooks
- Integration tests for component interactions
- E2E tests for critical user flows
- Mock data and fixtures for consistent testing

## Performance Considerations
- Code splitting with dynamic imports
- Image optimization and lazy loading
- Query optimization with selective field fetching
- Memoization for expensive computations
- Virtual scrolling for large lists

## Security Measures
- Row Level Security (RLS) in Supabase
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- XSS protection with proper sanitization
- CSRF protection through Supabase
- Secure token handling and storage
